business_type: Diversified holding company / insurance + operating businesses
red_flag: Buffett succession + record cash drag earning T-bill yields
why_interesting: Near 52wk-low offers reasonable entry to a fortress balance sheet with optionality from $300B+ cash pile; insurance float compounds at attractive incremental rates
reason_for_action: Analyzed 6 weeks ago (Own, 8.2); thesis intact, no >20% price move, well within 6-month freshness window.

business_type: Heavy machinery / construction + mining + power systems
red_flag: Backlog quality and end-market cyclicality if data-center capex normalizes
why_interesting: Q1 2026 EPS beat by ~20%, revenue +22% YoY, $63B record backlog, raised long-term CAGR target to 6-9% through 2030 driven by hyperscaler power-systems demand; flips earlier Pass thesis materially
reason_for_action: Prior verdict Pass (5.75) at March 2026; Q1 2026 beat + raised CAGR + hyperscaler power-systems backlog is a thesis-material change worth re-evaluating.

business_type: Mechanical / electrical contractor (HVAC, data center, industrial)
red_flag: Project-based revenue with cyclical risk if data-center capex slows
why_interesting: Fastest-growing pure-play on US data center HVAC + power buildout; record backlog, owner-operator culture, very high incremental ROIC; no prior analysis
reason_for_action: Highest-quality novel name with structural data-center tailwind; warrants full 8-umbrella treatment to size durability vs. cyclicality.

business_type: Search / digital advertising / cloud / AI platform
red_flag: AI search disruption + DOJ remedies overhang on default placements
why_interesting: Watch (7.25) from March 2026 nearing 8-week mark; fresh Q1 earnings + ongoing Gemini monetization + cloud growth deserve a thesis update
reason_for_action: Approaching freshness boundary with material AI/regulatory developments; recent earnings worth re-anchoring before next cycle.

red_flag: Housing turnover + DIY demand pressure if rates stay high
why_interesting: Watch (6.25) from March 2026; near 52wk-low offers improved setup; Pro contractor mix-shift thesis evolving
reason_for_action: 8 weeks since last analysis with near-52wk-low pricing dislocation; deserves re-anchoring to test whether MOS now sufficient.

red_flag: Stablecoin / account-to-account disintermediation tail risk
why_interesting: Own (8.1) from March 2026; near 52wk-low brings price closer to conservative IV ($346); duopoly economics intact
reason_for_action: Owned-quality name with material price dislocation since last analysis; refresh to confirm MOS and update for any new disintermediation signals.

red_flag: Content cost inflation + maturing subscriber growth narrative
why_interesting: Streaming leader with proven pricing power, ad-tier inflection, password-sharing tailwind; queue already in deep_research state — never been formally analyzed
reason_for_action: Already flagged for deep research; high-quality novel name worth full pipeline this cycle.

business_type: Trust bank / asset servicing + wealth management
red_flag: NIM sensitivity to rate cycle; sub-scale vs STT/BK in custody
why_interesting: Custody franchise with sticky AUC; conservative culture; reasonable valuation but trails BK/STT on scale economics
reason_for_action: Decent quality but custody peers offer better moat economics; monitor for clear price or operating-leverage catalyst.

red_flag: Volume/mix headwinds + private-label encroachment in mid-tier brands
why_interesting: Best-in-class staples portfolio with leading R&D and trade muscle; near 52wk-low improves setup; not yet analyzed
reason_for_action: High-quality novel name with price dislocation; warrants full pipeline to anchor a permanent watchlist position.

red_flag: Dupixent concentration + post-Opella spin execution risk
why_interesting: Dupixent franchise still expanding indications; cleaner pure-pharma post-consumer-spin; Roche/Novartis offer similar quality at similar valuation
reason_for_action: Quality is mid-tier among European pharma peers; ROG and NVS warrant deep dive priority first.

red_flag: Stablecoin / account-to-account disintermediation tail risk
why_interesting: Own (8.1) from March 2026; near 52wk-low closes the gap to conservative IV ($228); largest payment network globally
reason_for_action: Owned-quality name with material price dislocation since last analysis; refresh to confirm MOS and weigh stablecoin policy developments.

red_flag: Bioprocessing recovery slope still uncertain; China demand soft
why_interesting: Watch (6.9) from March 2026; bioprocessing destock cycle bottoming; DBS operating system still elite
reason_for_action: Analyzed 7 weeks ago; thesis intact, no >20% price move; monitor for bioprocessing inflection in next earnings cycle.

red_flag: Schenker integration execution + leverage step-up post-deal
why_interesting: Watch (6.6) from April 2026; Schenker integration is the entire thesis; too soon to re-evaluate
reason_for_action: Analyzed only 3 weeks ago; integration thesis needs more quarters to play out; monitor execution KPIs.

business_type: Life sciences instruments + bioproduction + lab products
red_flag: Pharma services demand recovery slow; tariff exposure on instruments
why_interesting: Watch (6.6) from March 2026; biotech funding recovery setup intact but not yet inflecting
reason_for_action: Analyzed 6 weeks ago; thesis intact, no >20% move; monitor for biotech demand recovery confirmation.

red_flag: DOJ criminal + civil Medicare billing probes; CEO turnover
why_interesting: Watch (6.25) from April 2026; price has plunged to ~$280 (below prior $300 conservative IV) on DOJ confirmation + Medicare reset; setup materially changed
reason_for_action: Material thesis-changing news (criminal DOJ probe confirmed, ~20% single-day drop) since April 18 analysis; refresh to re-test if regulatory haircut adequately priced.

red_flag: Cyclical short-cycle automation orders; FX translation drag
why_interesting: Pure-play electrification beneficiary (data center, grid, EV); margin step-up under Rosengren; SIE.DE is similar but bigger and more diversified
reason_for_action: High-quality compounder but valuation rich and SIE.DE is the priority European industrial automation deep_dive this cycle.

red_flag: M&A engine dependency + multiple compression risk if growth slows
why_interesting: Decentralized AMETEK Growth Model has produced consistent 12-15% capital allocation returns; not yet analyzed; cleaner exposure than ROP/ITW peers at this point
reason_for_action: High-quality decentralized compounder with novel coverage gap; warrants full pipeline.

business_type: Mid-cap branded consumer staples (Arm & Hammer, Trojan, OxiClean)
red_flag: Vitamin segment weakness + private-label competition in core categories
why_interesting: Capital-light brand portfolio with consistent FCF conversion; mid-cap exposure to staples without PG/KO megacap baggage
reason_for_action: Smaller, more cyclical brand exposure than PG; queue currently has it rejected — promote to monitor only if entry setup improves.

business_type: Commercial real estate data + analytics + marketplaces
red_flag: Homes.com profitability slip + heavy reinvestment depressing margins
why_interesting: Real estate data near-monopoly with 56 consecutive quarters of double-digit revenue growth; stock down ~30% from peak with Matterport now closed; setup looks improved
reason_for_action: Quality data monopoly with material drawdown setup; warrants full pipeline to test whether Homes.com losses are justified or capital destruction.

red_flag: Slowing license growth + cloud transition margin drag
why_interesting: Sticky PLM franchise in industrial verticals; high-quality recurring economics but valuation premium persists
reason_for_action: Quality franchise but expensive vs European software peers; SAP.DE and WKL.AS already analyzed cover similar exposure.

red_flag: Aspen+NI integration execution + Copeland JV dilution to focus thesis
why_interesting: Post-divestiture pure-play on automation software + process; integration story unfolding but unproven
reason_for_action: Transformation incomplete; SIE.DE and ABBN.SW offer cleaner read-throughs to industrial automation thesis.

red_flag: Freight cycle exposure + tariff/trade-flow volume risk
why_interesting: Owner-culture asset-light model with net cash, consistent buybacks, no debt; more conservative than DSV but smaller scale
reason_for_action: DSV.CO already analyzed covers asset-light freight forwarding; EXPD adds incremental but not differentiated exposure.

business_type: Industrial distribution / fasteners with Onsite + vending
red_flag: Manufacturing-cycle exposure + multiple-compression risk if PMI rolls
why_interesting: Onsite + FMI vending program is a distribution flywheel with very high incremental ROIC; consistent compounder; not yet analyzed and GWW comparison illuminating
reason_for_action: High-quality distribution compounder with structural Onsite tailwind; novel coverage; warrants full pipeline.

red_flag: AI/LLM disintermediation risk to terminal economics + Bloomberg encroachment
why_interesting: Above-budget: sticky data/terminal franchise with very high gross retention; share-of-wallet still expanding via workstation analytics; novel coverage. Prioritize in next cycle.
reason_for_action: Above-budget: quality recurring-revenue franchise deserving deep_dive but AI-disintermediation overhang weakens setup vs the other 8 priority deep_dives. Prioritize in next cycle.

red_flag: Endless Assortment margin dilution + e-comm pricing pressure
why_interesting: MRO scale moat with strong customer-stickiness; FAST is the priority distribution deep_dive this cycle given comparable economics and lower starting valuation
reason_for_action: FAST and GWW both cover industrial distribution; FAST takes the cycle slot, GWW monitored as alternative.

red_flag: Talc litigation tail + Stelara biosimilar erosion
why_interesting: AAA balance sheet with diversified pharma + medtech; valuation reasonable but no acute setup
reason_for_action: Quality holding-tier name; monitor for either talc resolution clarity or sharper pricing dislocation before deep_dive slot.

business_type: Branded beverage syrup concentrate / global franchise
red_flag: GLP-1 demand destruction risk for sugary beverages; FX translation
why_interesting: Iconic franchise with global pricing power; expensive at current multiple; PG offers cleaner consumer staples deep_dive
reason_for_action: Quality clearly there but valuation rich and PG is the consumer staples deep_dive priority this cycle.

red_flag: Capacity overhang post-pandemic + Vacaville integration execution
why_interesting: Pure-play biologics CDMO with multi-year capacity backlog; cycle slowly inflecting but execution still proving
reason_for_action: Watching for capacity-utilization confirmation; biologics CDMO scale story compelling but not yet inflecting.

red_flag: Low-income consumer traffic softness + GLP-1 cohort exposure
why_interesting: Best-in-class QSR franchise model with real-estate ballast; iconic brand; valuation full but durable
reason_for_action: High-quality but valuation full and no acute setup; monitor for sharper traffic dislocation or McValue inflection.

red_flag: NVDA customer concentration + multiple compression risk if AI capex normalizes
why_interesting: Highest-ROIC pure-play on AI server power-delivery + automotive; debt-free; consistent gross margin expansion
reason_for_action: Elite economics + AI infrastructure exposure + novel coverage; warrants full pipeline to size customer-concentration risk.

business_type: Diversified branded consumer staples (food, beverage, nutrition)
red_flag: Cocoa cost inflation + organic growth deceleration + governance turnover
why_interesting: Largest global branded staples portfolio; consistent dividend record; PG and KO peers preferred for cycle-priority
reason_for_action: Consumer staples deep_dive slot goes to PG; NESN remains a strong watchlist alternative.

red_flag: SMB headcount sensitivity to recession + Paycom/Gusto encroachment in mid-market
why_interesting: High-margin sticky payroll franchise; ADP analyzed already provides comparable read-through
reason_for_action: ADP already analyzed covers nearly identical thesis; PAYX adds limited differentiation, monitor as alternative.

red_flag: GLP-1 snack-volume risk + Frito margin pressure + tariff exposure
why_interesting: Snacks + beverage scale combination; Frito moat strong but slowing volumes; PG is the cycle priority
reason_for_action: Consumer staples deep_dive priority is PG; PEP remains a quality alternative on watchlist.

red_flag: Aerospace cycle exposure + leverage from Meggitt deal
why_interesting: Meggitt integration + aerospace aftermarket tailwind; capital allocation discipline strong; valuation full
reason_for_action: High quality but valuation premium; monitor for cycle softness creating better entry.

red_flag: Reinsurance cycle softening + concentration in specialty lines
why_interesting: Decade-plus track record of sub-90 combined ratios + special dividend culture; novel coverage; cleanest specialty-P&C compounder
reason_for_action: Elite underwriting record + owner-aligned culture + novel coverage; warrants full pipeline (queue currently rejected likely an error).

red_flag: Pipeline gaps post-Tecentriq + biosimilar erosion + obesity category late
why_interesting: Pharma + diagnostics combination at attractive multiple; obesity/Alzheimer pipeline catalysts pending
reason_for_action: Quality and valuation appealing but JNJ/NVS already analyzed; defer to next cycle pending obesity readouts.

red_flag: Premium valuation + slowing residential demand + roll-up pricing for tuck-ins rising
why_interesting: Recurring-revenue route-density model; high incremental ROIC; valuation persistently expensive
reason_for_action: High quality but valuation extended; monitor for cycle softness or larger drawdown before deep_dive slot.

red_flag: Conglomerate complexity + Siemens Healthineers stake overhang
why_interesting: Above-budget: European industrial automation + electrification leader with software (Mentor) + DI; SOTP-style discount narrative ongoing. Prioritize in next cycle.
reason_for_action: Above-budget: high-quality European industrial deserving deep_dive but FAST/AME/RLI/MPWR/CSGP/FDS/FIX/PG fill the budget. Prioritize in next cycle.

red_flag: Cantel integration legacy debt + EtO regulatory tightening on sterilization
why_interesting: Recurring sterilization moat with hospital-procedure volume tailwind; debt elevated but de-leveraging
reason_for_action: Quality moat but balance-sheet recovery still in progress; monitor for debt paydown milestones.

red_flag: Valuation re-rated to growth multiple + tariff cost-pass-through risk
why_interesting: Scale retailer with accelerating ad-tech + Walmart+ subscription monetization; valuation now full
